channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: This is what happened on March 12.
Surging Energy Costs Put German Industry ‘Really in Danger’
item.description: Four years after the Russian invasion of Ukraine sent energy prices soaring, the war in Iran is posing another challenge to efforts to revive European factories.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson and Andreas Meichsner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Carbon Caps and Emissions Trading Programs

item.title: Trump Removes Sanctions on Russia to Help Oil Flow Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said it was “unfortunate” that the move could benefit Russia, but maintained that it was only for the short term.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said on Thursday that Russia would not benefit significantly from the sanctions relief but acknowledged that it would see some financial benefit.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: Iran’s new supreme leader delivered a forceful message in his first public statement since succeeding his slain father, as the Israeli military bombarded Tehran and the Lebanese capital with strikes.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Beirut residents watching the aftermath of an Israeli airstrike in the Lebanese capital on Thursday.

item.title: U.S. KC-135 Refueling Plane Crashes in Iraq, Military Says
item.description: The crash was not because of hostile fire or friendly fire, U.S. Central Command said.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Greg Jaffe and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A U.S. KC-135 aircraft flying over Tel Aviv last week.

item.title: War in Iran Has India Scrambling to Keep Stoves Lit for Cooking
item.description: India relies on huge quantities of cooking gas that is normally shipped from the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Pragati K.B. and Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Workers cooked food using firewood instead of gas outside a restaurant in Bengaluru, India, amid disruptions in the liquid petroleum gas supply.

item.title: A Weakened Iran Hits Back by Strangling the Vital Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The threats to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz are complicating President Trump’s calculations about how and when to end the war.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski, Peter Eavis, Julian E. Barnes and Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: An oil tanker anchored in Muscat, Oman, on Thursday. Iran has throttled traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world’s oil was passing before the war began.

item.title: Netanyahu issues veiled threat to new supreme leader of Iran.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel speaking at a news conference at Mar-a-Lago in December.

item.title: Walid Khalidi, Scholar Called Father of Palestinian Studies, Dies at 100
item.description: As a historian and diplomat, he gave intellectual shape to his people and made sure that they played a role in negotiating their future.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

item.title: Iran Is Laying Mines in the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: A fifth of the world’s oil passes through the strait, making it a critical choke point in global commerce.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tanker anchored in Muscat, Oman, after Iran vowed to close the Strait of Hormuz last week.

item.title: What Is the Strait of Hormuz and Why Is Iran Blocking It?
item.description: With attacks and threats, Tehran is using the world’s most important transit point for oil and gas as leverage against its enemies.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: At least 16 oil tankers, cargo and other commercial ships have been attacked in or around the Persian Gulf since the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran began nearly two weeks ago, a New York Times analysis shows.

item.title: How Do You Feel About Traveling Right Now? We Want to Know.
item.description: With spring and summer travel season beginning amid a war in the Middle East, a partial government shutdown and more, we’d like to hear how your travel plans are changing.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.media:credit: Scott McIntyre for The New York Times
item.media:description: The war in Iran and headaches from an ongoing partial government shutdown have added a new level of complexity to vacation-planning season.

item.title: New Iranian Leader Vows to Keep Global Oil Gateway Blocked
item.description: Oil prices surged on Thursday after ships came under attack in the Persian Gulf, and Iran’s supreme leader vowed revenge for U.S. and Israeli airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati, Sanam Mahoozi and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An oil tanker burning after being hit by an Iranian strike near Basra, Iraq, late on Wednesday.

item.title: Israel Drops Case Against Soldiers Accused of Abusing Palestinian Detainee
item.description: After a prisoner arrived at a hospital with broken ribs and a torn rectum, Israelis were once again at odds over the issue of mistreatment and impunity.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Sde Teiman base in southern Israel, where former detainees and some Israeli soldiers have said that guards routinely abuse detained Gazans.

item.title: Iraq vents anger at strikes on former militias now under government control.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.media:description: Smoke rising from an attack at an airport in Erbil, Iraq, on Thursday.

item.title: Yanar Mohammed, 65, Iraqi Women’s Rights Advocate, Is Killed by Gunmen
item.description: She established a network of safe houses for abused women and was an outspoken critic of her country’s repressive institutions, despite the constant threat of violence.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:description: Yanar Mohammed in 2003. She co-founded the Organization of Women’s Freedom in Iraq to protect victims of rape, physical abuse and other forms of ill treatment.

item.title: Pardoning Netanyahu Now Would Be Improper, Key Israeli Office Says
item.description: Rebuffing pressure from President Trump, a legal office says the prime minister should be pardoned only if he resigns, confesses or is convicted.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu delivering a speech at the funeral for an Israeli hostage in January.

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s New Supreme Leader, Says Strait of Hormuz Must Remain Closed in Defiant Statement
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei struck a defiant tone and signaled that Iran would not back down in a war that has spread across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran depicts Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei taking an Iranian flag from his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Attacks on two tankers prompt Iraq to close oil terminals.
item.dc:creator: Josh Holder and Elena Shao

item.title: Displaced Lebanese Haunted by Strikes from the Sky
item.description: With airstrikes expanding beyond the limits of Beirut’s southern suburbs, people in the city say that even the once-safest corners may no longer be off-limits.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and Sarah Chaayto
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rising after airstrikes in southern suburbs of Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday.

item.title: Stryker Cyberattack Adds to Fears of New Front in Iran War
item.description: A hacking group seemed to claim responsibility for the attack on a U.S. manufacturer, calling it retaliation for a strike on an Iranian school.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Stryker, which makes medical equipment for customers in dozens of countries, said a cyberattack disrupted its systems on Wednesday.

item.title: War has sent thousands of planes flying in the other direction.
item.dc:creator: Zach Levitt and Jacqueline Gu

item.title: Closures of Middle East oil terminals add to jitters about prices.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.media:description: A vessel carrying Iraqi fuel oil near Basra, Iraq, on Thursday, after it was attacked by Iran.

item.title: Iran War Causing Largest Ever Oil Disruption, I.E.A. Says
item.description: Conflict is forcing producers to slash production and close ports as Iran steps up attacks on energy infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Frederic J. Brown/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: High prices at a gas station in Los Angeles on March 9, 2026, as costs have surged because of the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Ground Down by War, Hezbollah’s Loyal Base Shows Cracks
item.description: “We just want to be back in our homes,” said a Lebanese man who, like many others in the latest round of fighting, has to flee.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Displaced families, fleeing the fighting, in a tent camp inside a stadium in Beirut on Wednesday.

item.title: Iran’s Naval Mines: A History of Threats to Persian Gulf Shipping
item.description: The U.S. said this week that it attacked 16 Iranian mine-laying vessels near the Strait of Hormuz. In the 1980s, Iranian mines damaged oil tankers and a U.S. Navy warship.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The supertanker S.S. Bridgeton (top), escorted by the U.S.S. Standley, struck a mine off Dubai in November 1987.

item.title: How The Times Uses Graphics and Maps to Track the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Our visual journalists pinpoint attacks across the region and zoom in on individual strikes using satellite imagery.
item.dc:creator: Seth Carlson, Samuel Granados, Elena Shao and Lazaro Gamio
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How Hegseth Came to See Moral Purpose in War as Weakness
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s bellicose and vengeful rhetoric describing the military’s war in Iran grew out of his experience in Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth attending the dignified transfer of six troops killed in the Middle East last week. For the mission in Iran, Mr. Hegseth signed off on the name “Epic Fury,” which connotes retribution and rage.

item.title: Deadly Attack on Oil Tankers Prompts Iraq to Close Oil Terminals
item.description: An attack off the Iraqi coast engulfed two oil tankers in flames, killing at least one person. Senior Iraqi officials believe the attack was Iranian.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Media Office of Iraqi Ports, via Reuters

item.title: Here is the latest.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Julian E. Barnes, Euan Ward, Yeganeh Torbati and Johnatan Reiss

item.title: This is what happened on March 11.
Israel Bombards Lebanese Capital in Latest Round of Strikes
item.description: Lebanon’s health ministry said at least seven people were killed in the Beirut attacks early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack and Dayana Iwaza
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Dahiya, a densely populated residential area of southern Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday night.

item.title: U.S. to Release 172 Million Barrels of Oil From Strategic Reserves, Energy Dept. Says
item.description: The move aims to prevent prices from rising further because of the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: People filling up at a gas station in Los Angeles on Wednesday.

item.title: U.N. Security Council Condemns Iran’s Retaliatory Strikes in the Middle East
item.description: In an overwhelming vote, the council backed a resolution condemning Iran. A Russian proposal calling for an end to the war that didn’t assign blame or even name the parties, was rejected.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The United Nations Security Council during a meeting on Wednesday.

item.title: Countries Scramble to Shore Up Global Oil Supplies as Ships Are Attacked
item.description: The United States and Israel launched more strikes against Iran, where crowds mourned military commanders killed in the war. Israel also bombed targets in Lebanon, where the death toll climbed.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott, Abdi Latif Dahir, Eric Schmitt and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A navy ship from the United Arab Emirates sailed next to a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday.

item.title: Newsom Sees No Imminent Threat After F.B.I. Warning That Iranian Drones Could Attack California
item.description: Just before the war began, the Federal Bureau of Investigation told state officials that Iran might respond with drone attacks on California. No specific, credible threats have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett, Laurel Rosenhall, Soumya Karlamangla and Shawn Hubler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California speaking at a news conference in Hayward, Calif., last week.

item.title: What Happened in the U.S.-Israeli War on Iran on Wednesday
item.description: Israel and the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah in Lebanon traded strikes on Wednesday. Three ships were hit near the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil route.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The aftermath of an Israeli airstrike on Wednesday in Ruwais, a neighborhood in the southern suburbs of Beirut, Lebanon.

item.title: Strong or Weak? How Trump Picks His Battles.
item.description: Despite his tough talk, President Trump has consistently made allowances for countries he sees as powerful or dominant.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia during a meeting in Alaska last summer. Mr. Trump’s deference to Russia highlights a pattern that has crystallized in recent months as he launches military action overseas.

item.title: First Week of Iran War Cost More Than $11 Billion, Pentagon Tells Congress
item.description: In a Capitol Hill briefing, officials gave their most comprehensive assessment of the cost of the first six days of the war, but the number omitted several aspects of the operation.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A closed-door briefing at the Capitol with Pentagon officials discussed the cost of the war.

item.title: Banks in Gulf Evacuate Their Offices
item.description: After an overnight attack on a bank, Iranian officials signaled a new willingness to target economic centers and banks with ties to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland and Eshe Nelson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Citi, the global bank, told its staff to immediately depart what had been its longtime regional headquarters in the Dubai International Financial Center.

item.title: Lebanese residents are left in shock and fear as Israeli strikes reach the center of Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Chaayto and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.media:description: The recent Israeli strike killed four people according the Lebanese health ministry.

item.title: Iran Soccer Players Seeking Asylum Are Part of a History of Athlete Defections
item.description: Members of the Iranian soccer team who chose to remain in Australia this week are far from the first to travel to a competition and stay there.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather
item.media:credit: Dave Hunt/Australian Associated Press, via Via Reuters
item.media:description: Several members of the Iranian women’s soccer team accepted asylum offers from Australia after participating in the AFC Women’s Asian Cup.

item.title: Iran holds a public mourning ceremony for commanders killed by strikes.
item.description: Thousands of people mourned in a state-sanctioned ceremony, as people in Tehran described deepening anxiety and fear as the war continued.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati, Sanam Mahoozi and Leily Nikounazar
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The procession was held after another night of intense strikes.
